ID:       ddx-28f3cf37
Title:    federation: owner-targeted worker-control writes
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 3
Labels:   phase:2, area:federation, area:web, area:server, kind:test, story:14, spec:FEAT-026, ac-quality:needs-refinement
Parent:   ddx-a038a090
Deps:     ddx-0793ac75
Desc:     PROBLEM
`ddx-28f3cf37` was still framed as broad federation UI work, but the remaining gap is only the owner-targeted worker-control path: hub-issued `startWorker` and `stopWorker` must route to the owning spoke, refuse offline spokes, and preserve request-id idempotency.

ROOT CAUSE
`docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-026-federation.md:22-29,160-177` already defines the write-routing contract. `docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:327-338` isolates TC-017. `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-worker-control.spec.ts:1-9,102-214` already spells out the expected start/stop/offline/idempotency behavior.

PROPOSED FIX
- Narrow this bead to the worker-control owner-targeted write path.
- Track the Go-side gaps explicitly as `TestFederatedMutation_StartWorker_RoutesToOwner`, `TestFederatedMutation_StopWorker_RoutesToOwner`, `TestFederatedMutation_RefusesOfflineSpoke`, and `TestFederatedMutation_IdempotentRequestID`.
- Keep the bead aligned to FEAT-026 and TC-017 instead of reintroducing read-federation smoke.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add new `/federation` route read coverage.
- Do not expand this bead into bead/document write coverage.
- Do not add ts-net or cross-host networking beyond loopback.
Accept:   1. `ddx bead show ddx-28f3cf37` reads as a worker-control owner-targeted write bead, or the bead is closed/superseded.
2. The bead body names `TestFederatedMutation_StartWorker_RoutesToOwner`, `TestFederatedMutation_StopWorker_RoutesToOwner`, `TestFederatedMutation_RefusesOfflineSpoke`, and `TestFederatedMutation_IdempotentRequestID`.
3. The reconciliation notes cite `FEAT-026-federation.md:22-29,160-177`, `TP-002-server-web-ui.md:327-338`, and `federation-worker-control.spec.ts:1-9,102-214`.
4. The bead no longer points at the broad `/federation` UI story as the active scope.
Created:  2026-05-02 07:04:50
Updated:  2026-07-04 01:10:20

"PROBLEM
`ddx-28f3cf37` was still framed as broad federation UI work, but the remaining gap is only the owner-targeted worker-control path: hub-issued `startWorker` and `stopWorker` must route to the owning spoke, refuse offline spokes, and preserve request-id idempotency.

ROOT CAUSE
`docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-026-federation.md:22-29,160-177` already defines the write-routing contract. `docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:327-338` isolates TC-017. `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-worker-control.spec.ts:1-9,102-214` already spells out the expected start/stop/offline/idempotency behavior.

PROPOSED FIX
- Narrow this bead to the worker-control owner-targeted write path.
- Track the Go-side gaps explicitly as `TestFederatedMutation_StartWorker_RoutesToOwner`, `TestFederatedMutation_StopWorker_RoutesToOwner`, `TestFederatedMutation_RefusesOfflineSpoke`, and `TestFederatedMutation_IdempotentRequestID`.
- Keep the bead aligned to FEAT-026 and TC-017 instead of reintroducing read-federation smoke.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add new `/federation` route read coverage.
- Do not expand this bead into bead/document write coverage.
- Do not add ts-net or cross-host networking beyond loopback."

5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"709e64fba650c9c9a639d55ea4bc6ac928663669","created_at":"2026-07-03T22:45:12.978597568Z","description":"PROBLEM
`ddx-28f3cf37` still reads like a broad federation UI follow-up, but the real gap is the owner-targeted worker-control path. `docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:327-338` keeps TC-017 planned, while `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-026-federation.md:22-29,160-177` already defines the write-routing contract and `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-worker-control.spec.ts:1-9,102-214` already spells out the expected start/stop/offline/idempotency behavior at the UI level.

ROOT CAUSE
The current bead is still coupled to the broader federation UI story in `ddx-28f3cf37`, but the concrete contract is already isolated in FEAT-026 and the dedicated worker-control spec. What is missing is a narrower bead body that only tracks the live owner-targeted write-routing implementation and the Go-side tests that prove `startWorker` and `stopWorker` reach exactly one owning spoke.

PROPOSED FIX
- Rewrite or supersede `ddx-28f3cf37` so it covers only worker start/stop owner-targeted writes, not read-federation smoke.
- Name the missing implementation tests explicitly as `TestFederatedMutation_StartWorker_RoutesToOwner`, `TestFederatedMutation_StopWorker_RoutesToOwner`, `TestFederatedMutation_RefusesOfflineSpoke`, and `TestFederatedMutation_IdempotentRequestID`.
- Keep the bead aligned to FEAT-026 and TC-017 instead of duplicating the read-only federation tests.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add new `/federation` route read coverage.
- Do not touch bead/document writes here.
- Do not add ts-net or cross-host networking beyond the local loopback gate.
- Do not modify the FEAT-026 text.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:23:{"acceptance":"1. `ddx bead show ddx-359e812b` is narrowed to live owner-targeted write persistence or is closed/superseded.
2. The bead body names `TestFederatedBeadWrite_ForwardsToOwner`, `TestFederatedDocumentWrite_ForwardsToOwner`, `TestFederatedWrite_RejectsOfflineSpoke`, and `TestFederatedWrite_IdempotentRequestID`.
3. The reconciliation notes cite `TP-002-server-web-ui.md:340-351,353-363`, `beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts:210-268`, `spec-editing.spec.ts:204-256`, and `federation-2node.spec.ts:219-307`.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/graphql/... ./internal/federation/...` passes, and `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bunx playwright test e2e/beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts e2e/spec-editing.spec.ts` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","created_at":"2026-07-03T22:45:13.050927785Z","description":"PROBLEM
`ddx-359e812b` is still too broad for what remains. `docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:340-351,353-363` keeps bead, spec, and document writes planned, but the current Playwright suite already covers the mocked forwarded paths in `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts:210-268` and `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/spec-editing.spec.ts:204-256`, while `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts:219-307` already covers the read-federation shell around the owner-write entry points.

ROOT CAUSE
The stale bead conflates three concerns: hub/spoke federation reads, mocked UI write-forwarding, and the live owner-targeted persistence gap. FEAT-026 says forwarding is owner-targeted and never broadcast (`docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-026-federation.md:22-29,160-177`), so the bead should only track the missing live bead/document write persistence and refusal behavior, not the already-covered smoke paths.

PROPOSED FIX
- Rewrite or supersede `ddx-359e812b` so it narrows to live owner-targeted bead create/edit and documentWrite persistence.
- Keep the ts-net guard as a separate child if it is still load-bearing.
- Add or update the missing owner-write tests as `TestFederatedBeadWrite_ForwardsToOwner`, `TestFederatedDocumentWrite_ForwardsToOwner`, `TestFederatedWrite_RejectsOfflineSpoke`, and `TestFederatedWrite_IdempotentRequestID`.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add new federation read smoke.
- Do not add screenshot assertions.
- Do not implement managed-node outbound control from ADR-028.
- Do not change the mocked Playwright smoke paths unless they are being split out of this bead.
